triageFlags.title: Credit Score in Sub-Prime Tier
triageFlags.description: The applicant's credit score places them in the Sub-Prime category, requiring stricter lending criteria.
triageFlags.recommendation: Ensure all other aspects of the proposal meet the stricter requirements for Sub-Prime applicants.
triageFlags.reasoning: According to the policy, a credit score of 575 falls into Tier C (Sub-Prime), which necessitates additional scrutiny and stricter lending criteria.

triageFlags.title: Loan-to-Value Ratio Exceeds Tier C Limit
triageFlags.description: The Loan-to-Value ratio is close to the maximum allowable limit for Tier C applicants.
triageFlags.recommendation: Verify the accuracy of the vehicle's trade price and ensure the advance amount is justified.
triageFlags.reasoning: The policy specifies a maximum LTV of 95% for Tier C applicants. The current LTV is within this limit but requires careful validation due to its proximity to the threshold.

content.reasoning: The applicant's credit score places them in the Sub-Prime category, necessitating stricter lending criteria. The DTI ratio is within acceptable limits for this tier, but the high Loan-to-Value ratio and the applicant's credit history warrant a cautious approach.
